relationships.description: Chickpea exhibits tolerance to terminal drought.
Rooting was initially hypothesized to be critical for drought tolerance.
Water use pattern is critical for drought tolerance.
Root depth was measured in relation to drought tolerance.
Root density was measured in relation to drought tolerance.
Yield is used as a basis for assessing drought tolerance.
Genotypes were selected based on their terminal drought-tolerance index.
Water extraction patterns differentiated tolerant and sensitive genotypes.
Tolerant genotypes had lower stomatal conductance at the vegetative stage.
Tolerant genotypes extracted more water after flowering.
Root growth components did not distinguish tolerant from sensitive genotypes.
Seed yield was not correlated with root length density.
Seed yield was negatively related to water uptake between 23-38 DAS.
Seed yield was positively related to water uptake between 48-61 DAS.
Lower canopy conductance leads to conservative water use.
Conservative water use leads to more water available for reproduction.
Increased water availability during reproduction leads to higher reproductive success and yield.
